concepts.name: Function
concepts.short_definition: A relation between a set of inputs and a set of permissible outputs with the property that each input is related to exactly one output.

concepts.name: Domain
concepts.short_definition: The set of all possible input values for a function.

concepts.name: Range
concepts.short_definition: The set of all possible output values for a function.

concepts.name: Real Number
concepts.short_definition: A value that represents a quantity along a continuous line.

concepts.name: Interval
concepts.short_definition: A set of real numbers that lie between two given numbers.

concepts.name: Open Interval
concepts.short_definition: An interval that does not include its endpoints.

concepts.name: Closed Interval
concepts.short_definition: An interval that includes its endpoints.

concepts.name: Limit of a Function
concepts.short_definition: The value that a function approaches as the input approaches some value.

concepts.name: Limit Point
concepts.short_definition: A point such that every open interval containing it also contains a point of the set other than the point itself.

concepts.name: Epsilon-Delta Definition
concepts.short_definition: A formal definition of a limit using epsilon and delta.

concepts.name: Continuity at a Point
concepts.short_definition: A function is continuous at a point if its limit at that point exists and equals the function's value at that point.

concepts.name: Continuity on an Interval
concepts.short_definition: A function is continuous on an interval if it is continuous at every point in that interval.

concepts.name: Discontinuity
concepts.short_definition: A point where a function is not continuous.

concepts.name: Removable Discontinuity
concepts.short_definition: A discontinuity that can be removed by redefining the function at a single point.

concepts.name: Jump Discontinuity
concepts.short_definition: A discontinuity where the left-hand limit and right-hand limit exist but are not equal.

concepts.name: Infinite Discontinuity
concepts.short_definition: A discontinuity where the function approaches infinity or negative infinity.

concepts.name: Oscillating Discontinuity
concepts.short_definition: A discontinuity where the function oscillates infinitely often near a point.

concepts.name: Left-Hand Limit
concepts.short_definition: The limit of a function as the input approaches a value from the left side.

concepts.name: Right-Hand Limit
concepts.short_definition: The limit of a function as the input approaches a value from the right side.

concepts.name: Intermediate Value Theorem
concepts.short_definition: States that for a continuous function on an interval, any value between the function's values at the endpoints is attained by the function at some point within the interval.

concepts.name: Extreme Value Theorem
concepts.short_definition: States that a continuous function on a closed interval attains both a maximum and a minimum value on that interval.

concepts.name: Uniform Continuity
concepts.short_definition: A stronger form of continuity where the epsilon-delta condition is independent of the point being considered.

concepts.name: Continuous Function Properties
concepts.short_definition: Rules and theorems that apply to functions that are continuous.

concepts.name: Sum of Continuous Functions
concepts.short_definition: The sum of two continuous functions is also continuous.

concepts.name: Product of Continuous Functions
concepts.short_definition: The product of two continuous functions is also continuous.

concepts.name: Quotient of Continuous Functions
concepts.short_definition: The quotient of two continuous functions is continuous wherever the denominator is non-zero.

concepts.name: Composition of Continuous Functions
concepts.short_definition: The composition of two continuous functions is also continuous.

concepts.name: Polynomial Function
concepts.short_definition: A function consisting of terms with non-negative integer exponents.

concepts.name: Rational Function
concepts.short_definition: A function that can be written as the ratio of two polynomial functions.

concepts.name: Trigonometric Function
concepts.short_definition: Functions that relate an angle of a right-angled triangle to ratios of side lengths.

concepts.name: Exponential Function
concepts.short_definition: A function where the variable appears in the exponent.

concepts.name: Logarithmic Function
concepts.short_definition: The inverse function of an exponential function.

concepts.name: Piecewise Function
concepts.short_definition: A function defined by multiple sub-functions, each applying to a certain interval of the main function's domain.

concepts.name: Continuity of Polynomials
concepts.short_definition: Polynomial functions are continuous everywhere.

concepts.name: Continuity of Rationals
concepts.short_definition: Rational functions are continuous on their domains.

concepts.name: Continuity of Trigonometrics
concepts.short_definition: Standard trigonometric functions are continuous on their domains.

concepts.name: Continuity of Exponentials
concepts.short_definition: Exponential functions are continuous everywhere.

concepts.name: Continuity of Logarithms
concepts.short_definition: Logarithmic functions are continuous on their domains.

concepts.name: Continuity of Piecewise Functions
concepts.short_definition: Continuity of piecewise functions must be checked at the boundary points between sub-functions.
